quotes.text: Malachi (Mal. 1:1). "My messenger." Signifies Malachi's calling as a prophet-messenger.
quotes.attr: Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Donald W. Parry, A Guide to Scriptural Symbols, p.190

notes: Jacob stood as a symbol for Israel or the chosen people while Esau (Edom) symbolized the world.
polluted thee--that is, offered to thee "polluted bread."

quotes.text: The altar, or "table of the Lord," as Malachi called it (Malachi 1:7), was the place of intercession, peacemaking, expiation, and sanctification. That which was consumed by its flames had in a figurative sense been consumed by God and was therefore under stood to have been accepted by him.
quotes.speaker: Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie
quotes.attr: Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie, Gospel Symbolism, p.104

quotes.text: It was also sometimes called the whole burnt offering because the whole animal was consumed upon the altar. The altar was "the table of the Lord" (Mal. 1:7, 12; Ps. 23:5) and whatever was put upon it was the "food of God" (Lev. 21:6, 8, 17, 21).
quotes.speaker: Victor L. Ludlow
quotes.attr: Victor L. Ludlow, Unlocking the Old Testament, p.34

notes: The priests and Levites of Malachi's day were mocking God by offering sacrifices to the Lord with sick, blind, and lame animals and calling them acceptable

quotes.text: No ritual performance is acceptable to the Lord that is not sustained by righteousness on the part of those participating in it (see Malachi 1:10). If the ritual of baptism be the example, it is of none effect unless it be accompanied by true repentance.
quotes.source: Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon
quotes.attr: Joseph Fielding Mc Conkie and Robert L. Millet, Doctrinal Commentary on the Book of Mormon, 4 vols., 2:, p.210

quotes.text: This should rather be translated as a wish: 'Oh that there were even one among you that would shut the doors, that ye might not kindle fire on mine altar in vain.'
quotes.speaker: Ellis Rasmussen
quotes.attr: Ellis Rasmussen, Introduction to the Old Testament 2:110

notes: Edom was not alone in her wickedness. The people of Judah, and particularly the Levites living among them, were also polluted and corrupt. As the spiritual sons and servants of the Lord (see v. 6), their offerings to God had become common and worthless. Inasmuch as the sacrifices that they made for the people typified the coming sacrifice and atonement of the Son of God, the only acceptable sacrifice was
2:1-17. REPROOF OF THE PRIESTS FOR VIOLATING THE COVENANT; AND THE PEOPLE ALSO FOR MARRIAGES OUTSIDE THE COVENANT AND UNFAITHFULNESS.

quotes.text: Jesus Christ is the Lord of Hosts or the Leader and King of the hosts of heaven as well as the righteous hosts of Israel here upon the earth (1 Chron. 17:24; Isa. 6: 5). "I am a great King, saith the Lord of hosts, and my name is dreadful among the heathen" (Mal. 1:14). Thus, to the righteous, the appearance of the Lord of Hosts is a joyful occasion, but the wicked tremble and seek to hide themselves when he manifests himself (Rev. 6:15-16).
quotes.speaker: Hoyt W. Brewster
quotes.attr: Hoyt W. Brewster, Jr., Doctrine and Covenants Encyclopedia, p.329
